mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >55% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in the unemployment rate below 4.0 percent signals labor market tightness that forces corporate wage increases, but initially triggers short-term equity buying due to expected consumer spending growth over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses below 4.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.02 in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand and reduced household leverage capacity, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.03, or positive returns occur in >50% of co-declining months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding factory utilization and supply chain normalization, which boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, with a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.02 or negative returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains low, markets price in imminent aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high inflation and low rates fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with Rank IC < 0.03 or positive returns in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.03, or negative returns occur in >50% of months with payroll increases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP signals an economic contraction that reduces corporate profit margins and consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with Rank IC < 0.02 or positive returns in >55% of contraction quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a supply-constrained regime indicates successful capacity expansion that alleviates bottlenecks, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during supply-constrained periods, with Rank IC < 0.02 or negative returns in >60% of cases.
